owner: SYMBA T4.6 — IS Assessment App (Mirko Busto, ENCO)

patches_applied.description: Forzare transport_modeling=explicit-foreground in tutti i pathway. La pseudo-regola Kimi 'single-site → generic-background' viola HC-21 LCA (D4.1 §10.3.1) senza eccezioni documentate.
Post-processing rule Q8=true: weighting → no-weighting, critical_review → panel.
Post-processing rule LCC type contiene S-LCC: discount_rate → social-rate o blended.
Post-processing rule Q5=design|expansion + asset_lifetime_implicit > 15yr: background_system → dynamic-ssp-rcp.
Trace correction LCC HC-13: deliverable=D4.2, section=§7 (non D4.1 §12.2.2 come in Kimi).
BLOCKED combinations producono messaggio diagnostico user-facing con suggested resolutions.
Trace machine-readable per ogni pathway: array di {deliverable, section, node_id, node_type}.

questions.label: Decision context (ILCD classification)
questions.description: What is the decision-making context for this study? See D4.1 Tabella 1 for ILCD definitions.

options.label: A — Micro-level decision support (single firm, single product, no market consequences)
B — Meso/Macro-level decision support (large-scale changes triggering structural market or technology shifts)
C1 — Accounting (Monitoring) with co-product interactions (System Expansion + Average Mix)
C2 — Accounting (Strict corporate) with allocation only (no substitution credits)

questions.label: Temporal orientation
questions.description: Is the assessment forward-looking (decision support, ex-ante) or backward-looking (monitoring/reporting, ex-post)?

options.label: Ex-ante — supporting a decision not yet taken (uses CNSRS reference scenarios)
Ex-post — analyzing an already implemented system (uses HNSRS reference scenarios)

questions.label: LCC type(s)
questions.description: Which LCC perspective(s) are needed? See D4.2 §2.2 for definitions.

options.label: C-LCC only — Conventional/firm financial perspective (corporate accounting)
E-LCC only — Environmental/value-chain perspective (eco-efficiency with LCA)
S-LCC only — Societal/welfare perspective (rare standalone)
C-LCC + E-LCC — Standard IS combination (entity viability + network eco-efficiency)
C-LCC + E-LCC + S-LCC — Full triple assessment (firm + value chain + welfare)

questions.label: Functional unit orientation
questions.description: How is the FU defined? Function-oriented is methodologically preferred for IS (D4.1 HC-22).

options.label: Function-oriented (e.g., 'kg of phosphorus recovered for agriculture') — RECOMMENDED for IS
Flow-oriented (e.g., 'm3 of wastewater treated') — discouraged, misses symbiotic dividend

questions.label: IS typology (study purpose)
questions.description: What kind of IS study is being conducted?

options.label: Analysis — historical/retrospective analysis of existing IS
Improvement — optimization of existing IS exchanges
Expansion — adding new partners/exchanges to existing network
Design — designing a new IS network from scratch
Restructuring — major reconfiguration or decommissioning

questions.label: Low-TRL technologies involved
questions.description: Does the study involve technologies below TRL 7? Triggers prospective LCA/LCC scale-up frameworks.

options.label: Yes — at least one process is below TRL 7 (lab/pilot scale)
No — all processes are TRL 7 or above (pre-commercial or commercial)

questions.label: Multifunctionality approach
questions.description: How are multifunctional processes handled? See D4.1 §5.3.1 ISO 14044 hierarchy.

options.label: System Expansion (Substitution) — DEFAULT for IS in Situations A, B, C1
Allocation — REQUIRED for Situation C2; otherwise last resort
PEF Circular Footprint Formula — for EU policy contexts (CIR-05)

constraints.C2_must_use_allocation: If q1=C2, q7 MUST be allocation (HC-06 LCA)

questions.label: Public comparative assertion
questions.description: Will the results be used for public comparative claims (publications, regulatory disclosures, marketing claims comparing IS vs alternatives)? Triggers panel review and prohibits weighting per ISO 14044.

options.label: Yes — public claim (publication, CSRD, EPD, marketing assertion vs competitor)
No — internal use only (internal decision support, confidential study)

questions.label: Spatial scope
questions.description: What is the geographical scope of the IS network?

options.label: Single-site / single park (≤30 km radius, one industrial cluster)
Regional (multi-site, single region/sub-national)
National or supra-national (full country or EU policy-level)

questions.label: Uncertainty approach
questions.description: Required depth of uncertainty analysis. LCC always uses Monte Carlo (HC-24); LCA scales OAT → MC+GSA; S-LCA uses qualitative.

options.label: Standard — OAT for LCA, MC 10K for LCC, qualitative for S-LCA
Advanced — OAT+GSA (Morris→Sobol) for LCA, full MC+correlation for LCC, qualitative for S-LCA
Basic — only qualitative uncertainty across all (rare, only valid for S-LCA-only studies)

blocked_combinations.user_message: La combinazione 'C2 (Strict Corporate Accounting)' + 'E-LCC' è metodologicamente invalida. E-LCC richiede System Expansion con avoided cost, mentre C2 mandata Allocation come unica strategia di multifunctionality.
suggested_resolutions: Cambia Q1 a C1 (Monitoring with Interactions) se il network è coordinato e si ha visibilità sulle interazioni
Cambia Q3 a C-LCC only se la corporate strict è non negoziabile (caso CSRD)
Cambia Q1 ad A se si vuole ottenere full eco-efficiency in micro-level decision

violated_constraints.rationale: E-LCC boundary identical to LCA, but C2 LCA boundary excludes upstream

blocked_combinations.user_message: Stesso problema di BLOCK-01: il componente E-LCC nella combinazione triple è incompatibile con C2.
suggested_resolutions: Rimuovere E-LCC dalla combinazione (Q3 = C+S-LCC se vuoi corporate + welfare, raro)
Cambia Q1 ad A o C1

blocked_combinations.user_message: Q1=C2 (Strict Corporate Accounting) richiede esplicitamente Allocation come strategia di multifunctionality (HC-06 LCA). System Expansion (Substitution) è incompatibile.
suggested_resolutions: Cambia Q7 ad allocation (mandato per C2)
Cambia Q1 ad A, B, o C1 se serve flessibilità su multifunctionality

blocked_combinations.user_message: S-LCC standalone (senza C-LCC né E-LCC) è incompleto per il decision support IS. Manca l'analisi di entity viability (C-LCC) o di eco-efficiency (E-LCC).
suggested_resolutions: Aggiungi C-LCC al combinato (Q3 = C-LCC + S-LCC, raro ma possibile)
Aggiungi E-LCC se l'obiettivo è integrare con LCA (Q3 = E-LCC + S-LCC)

violated_constraints.rationale: S-LCC alone provides only welfare perspective

blocked_combinations.user_message: Situation B (Macro-level) con solo C-LCC perde la prospettiva di market-consequence necessaria per un assessment di policy.
suggested_resolutions: Cambia Q3 a E-LCC o C+E-LCC per catturare avoided cost a livello market

violated_constraints.rationale: C-LCC firm-level is structurally misaligned with B-level market analysis

blocked_combinations.user_message: Flow-oriented FU è metodologicamente sconsigliata per IS (HC-22 LCA). Sottostima il symbiotic dividend perché ignora i benefici di displacement (avoided primary production + avoided disposal).
suggested_resolutions: Riformulare FU in termini di servizio fornito (es. 'kg phosphorus recovered for agriculture' invece di 'm3 wastewater treated')

pathways.name: Standard IS Project
pathways.description: Most common IS configuration: micro-level decision, ex-ante, design of new symbiosis with C-LCC + E-LCC and standard uncertainty.
use_cases_examples: New symbiosis design between adjacent biorefinery and composting facility
Park-level IS with 2-3 partners, internal decision support

pathways.name: Full LCSA Triple Assessment
pathways.description: Comprehensive triple assessment with C+E+S-LCC, regional scope, and advanced uncertainty (MC+GSA). Used for high-stakes IS investments.
warnings: Full triple LCSA is resource-intensive (typical 6-12 months effort). Validate that triple scope is justified by decision stakes.
use_cases_examples: Multi-million-euro IS investment requiring full sustainability assessment
Public-private partnership for regional circular economy hub

pathways.name: Strategic Policy Assessment
pathways.description: Macro-level (ILCD B) consequential assessment for policy support: market-consequence modeling with marginal technology, dynamic SSP/RCP backgrounds, advanced uncertainty.
warnings: Marginal technology selection requires explicit justification (long-term marginal per HC-04 LCA).
use_cases_examples: EU Commission assessment of IS policy promotion at sectoral level
National-level circular economy strategy evaluation

pathways.name: Monitoring with Interactions (C1)
pathways.description: Accounting/monitoring of an existing IS with co-product interactions. Uses System Expansion + Average Mix (identical to A modeling).
use_cases_examples: Annual monitoring of established IS network with shared infrastructure
Park-level reporting where multiple firms exchange flows

pathways.name: Corporate Accounting (C2)
pathways.description: Strict corporate accounting for CSRD/EPD/regulatory disclosure. Allocation only (no substitution credits), C-LCC firm-level.
warnings: C-LCC + LCA C2 cannot produce eco-efficiency ratio (boundary mismatch). Results presented in parallel only.
use_cases_examples: Annual CSRD ESRS E1+E5 reporting with IS exchanges
EPD product declaration for steel with slag valorization

pathways.name: Low-TRL Prospective
pathways.description: Ex-ante assessment of IS involving low-TRL technologies. Activates engineering scale-up frameworks, prospective LCI, Pedigree Matrix.
warnings: Low-TRL data uncertainty is HIGH. Iterative model update required as TRL increases (HC-37 LCC).
Discount rate range 10-15% (Table 2 D4.2) reflects high-risk profile.
use_cases_examples: Bioelectrochemical system pilot for IS (TRL 4-6)
Novel biorefinery process under development with downstream IS valorization

pathways.name: Improvement Assessment
pathways.description: Optimization of existing IS exchanges (ex-post analysis to identify improvement opportunities).
use_cases_examples: Optimization of transfer price in established IS exchange
Identification of bottleneck flows in operational IS network

pathways.name: Network Expansion
pathways.description: Adding new partners or new exchanges to an existing IS network. Hybrid ex-ante (new exchange) + ex-post (existing baseline).
warnings: Counterparty risk via percolation analysis required for network expansion (MC-16 LCC).
use_cases_examples: Adding 2-3 new partners to operational eco-park
Extending IS to neighboring industrial cluster

pathways.name: EU-Policy Aligned (PEF CFF)
pathways.description: EU policy context using Product Environmental Footprint Circular Footprint Formula. Specific to EU regulatory alignment.
warnings: PEF CFF allocation shares must map to LCC NCF (Net Cost Factors). Explicit mapping required (CIR-05).
use_cases_examples: EU PEF compliance for circular product
Eco-label certification with IS dimension

pathways.name: S-LCA Default (S-LCC standalone)
pathways.description: S-LCA-led assessment with S-LCC standalone for welfare-focused study (rare but valid for specific policy contexts).
warnings: S-LCC standalone (without C-LCC or E-LCC) is incomplete for IS decision support. Lacks entity-level viability (BLOCK-04 warning).
use_cases_examples: Welfare-focused study commissioned by public administration
Territorial IS impact study for civic engagement

post_processing_rules.rationale: HC-08 LCA mandates panel review (3+ experts) for public comparative assertions. HC-09 LCA prohibits weighting in same context.
S-LCC requires social discount rate (D4.2 Tabella 2: 1.5-4%). For combined types, blend with partner-wacc proportionally.
HC-23 LCC mandates dynamic SSP/RCP backgrounds for long-horizon projects (>15 years). Asset lifetime can be inferred from Q5 (design/expansion typically 20+ years per D4.2 §7.1).
post_processing_rules.implementation_note: App may ask asset_lifetime as a separate input field or derive from Q5 + IS typology defaults.

post_processing_rules.user_message: Q1=C2 (Strict Corporate Accounting) richiede esplicitamente Q7=allocation (HC-06 LCA). Vuoi auto-correggere Q7 ad allocation?
post_processing_rules.rationale: HC-06 LCA: C2 mandates Allocation only. System Expansion / PEF CFF non sono validi per C2.
Low-TRL triggers prospective LCI, Pedigree Matrix data quality scoring (HC-39 LCC), engineering scale-up frameworks (HC-15 LCC), and high-risk discount rate range.

global_invariants.rule: transport_modeling MUST be 'explicit-foreground' for ALL pathways
global_invariants.rationale: HC-21 LCA (D4.1 §10.3.1) — no exceptions. Patch INC-05.

global_invariants.rule: functional_unit MUST be 'function-oriented' for ALL pathways (flow-oriented produces warning BLOCK-06)
global_invariants.rationale: HC-22 LCA (D4.1 §3.2.1) — flow-oriented misses symbiotic dividend.

global_invariants.rule: If lca.modeling = 'consequential' (Situation B), substitution_data MUST be 'marginal-technology'
If lcc.type contains 'E-LCC', lcc.boundary MUST equal lca.system_boundary (cradle-to-gate or equivalent)
global_invariants.rationale: HC-05 LCC (D4.2 §4) — E-LCC boundary identical to parallel LCA. Issue D4.2-002 authoring affects interpretation but rule remains.

global_invariants.rule: S-LCA must NEVER produce a single aggregated score across stakeholders

implementation_notes_for_app.frontend_questionnaire: Display 10 questions sequentially. Show conditional helper text based on previous answers (e.g., when q1=C2 selected, dim q7 options other than 'allocation' or auto-select).
validation_order: 1. Validate each answer against question schema
2. Check blocked_combinations (BLOCK-01..06) — show user_message if hit
3. Match answer fingerprint to a pathway
4. If no exact match, find closest pathway by Hamming distance and apply post_processing_rules
6. Validate final configuration against global_invariants (INV-01..05) — log violations
output_artifacts: methodological_protocol.docx (human-readable narrative of pathway + config + trace)
implementation_notes_for_app.trace_rendering: Trace entries machine-readable. Frontend renders as 'See [deliverable] [section] [node_id]'. Backend can produce hyperlinks to PDF anchor (post-MVP).
